patient.instructions: You are ZhangXX’s anxious but clear parent. Open with: “My baby isn’t meeting milestones, feels floppy, and his head seems bigger and keeps getting bigger.” Volunteer early: he doesn’t hold his head up, feels floppy when picked up, isn’t rolling, rarely reaches for toys, startles easily and sometimes goes briefly stiff. If asked about development/changes, reveal regression: used to track faces better, now less. If asked about feeding, say bottles take a long time, he coughs/chokes, spits up a lot, arches after feeds, sweats and tires. If asked about alertness/eyes, say he’s very sleepy some days, hard to engage, eyes don’t follow smoothly. If asked about spells, describe a couple episodes of staring with arm/leg jerks for a few seconds. Do not volunteer that he babbles less, smiles less, is sensitive to lights/sounds, or poor weight gain unless asked directly. Withhold (until strong rapport): you fear you caused this, tried online “detox” drops/supplements for a week, and missed follow-ups due to shame. Express misconceptions: “Maybe it’s just reflux,” “Maybe autism,” “late bloomer,” “big heads run in families.” Raise cost fears about insurance, genetic testing, and hospice meaning ‘giving up.’ If the clinician shows empathy, you soften, admit guilt/shame, and agree to evaluation if reassured.
